violations.text: Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of black residue and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Unit taken out of service to be thoroughly cleaned and sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 04/13/2026

Working containers of salt, etc. are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 04/13/2026
The non-food contact surfaces of the scoop holders on dry food storage bins have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 04/14/2026

An area of the basement has an accumulation of standing water. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. PIC informed to clean, sanitize and monitor area. New Violation. Correct By: 04/13/2026
The hand-washing sink in the upstairs bar/server area was lacking paper towels. Staff provided during inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/23/2024

Eggplant and cheese were observed held at 48 degrees Fahrenheit in the fry-area prep unit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC replaced cover to prep unit, inspector obtained an ambient of 43 degrees. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/23/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Gnocchi were observed cooling in covered, stacked containers in the walk-in. PIC uncovered containers during inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/23/2024
Inspector observed a leak at a wastewater pipe in the basement (near basement entrance). New Violation. Correct By: 08/19/2024

The floor and wall junctures throughout the kitchen do not have coving. The floor and wall junctures must be coved and closed to no larger than 1/32nd of an inch. PIC stated that coving is scheduled to be installed tomorrow. New Violation. Correct By: 09/03/2024

An employee was observed touching ready to eat food (basil leaves) with bare hands. Food employees must not touch exposed, ready-to-eat food with their bare hands and shall use suitable utensils such as deli tissue, spatulas, single use gloves, or dispensing equipment. New Violation. Correct By: 07/23/2024
The hand-washing sink in the upstairs bar/server station. was lacking paper towels. New Violation. Correct By: 07/23/2024
Hand washing sinks throughout the facility are lacking proper signage. New Violation. Correct By: 08/06/2024

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the downstairs bar dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. Service arrived during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/23/2024

Several foods were observed held at 48-50 degrees Fahrenheit in the prep units. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Chef had staff place foods on ice in prep unit during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/23/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Gnocchi were observed cooling in covered, stacked containers in the walk-in. Chef uncovered so that the pasta could cool. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/23/2024
Boxes of food were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in freezer. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 08/06/2024

Bowls are being used as scoops in containers of dry goods. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. New Violation. Correct By: 07/23/2024

The ice scoop is stored on an unclean surface (top of the ice machine) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. PIC took ice scoop to be cleaned and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/23/2024

The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine and quaternary ammonium sanitizing solutions for the dish machines and 3-bay sinks. Service technician provided test strips while repairing dish machine. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/23/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 08/06/2024
The wall in the kitchen near the 3-bay sink area is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/27/2023

The floor and wall junctures in the throughout the kitchen do not have coving. The floor and wall junctures must be coved and closed to no larger than 1/32nd of an inch. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/03/2024
